claim: The committed scorecards are GRADUATED (magnitude-computed), NOT a hardcoded constant.
scorer: 23-ai-workforce-blueprint/full-funnel-pipeline/funnel_rubrics.py (the SINGLE canonical scorer; score_rubrics.py in this tree is a thin adapter that calls it).
method: Re-run the canonical funnel_rubrics.score_all against COPIES of THIS live FocusForge evidence tree with one sub-check's evidence degraded; observe the score change. A binary 'hi if ok else lo' card could only ever emit hi or lo — these intermediate, distinct values cannot be produced by a constant.
hard_miss_gate: A sub-check with weight >= 3.0 that earns 0 fails the rubric outright (proven by the funnel_type drop: score 7.0 AND passed=false), so a missing load-bearing artifact cannot be averaged away into a pass.
